control.content:
General
Features
Content
Single Episode Lists
Meta Accounts
Sources Accounts
Results
Playback '

item.property: Autostart Red Light When Kodi Starts
Enable this and Red Light will autostart after running its services upon Kodi start
Choose any custom background you would like to use within Red Light

Enable this and Red Light will limit the threads enacted simultaneously. Best to leave this disabled unless you are having issues with your device not supporting high numbers of concurrent threads in python
Choose the maximum active concurrent threads Red Light will be limited to
Choose the theme Red Light will use for custom windows. Choices are Light, Medium and Dark
Choose the Opacity Red Light will use for custom window themes. Choose between 10% - 100%
Choose the provider that keeps track of the Movies and Episodes you have watched within Red Light. Options are the built in method of Red Light or your Trakt account
Choose how often Red Light polls your Trakt service for any changes
Enable this and Red Light will refresh widgets after performing a Trakt Sync if there were any changes
Choose the correct UTC offset based on your location
Choose your download location for Movies
Choose your download location for TV Shows
Choose your download location for Premium files. Includes downloads from debrid service caches or EasyNews
Choose your download location for Images
Choose what content is included within the Extras window
Choose how the content within the Extras window is ordered
Choose the function and order of the buttons in the Extras window. You can set separate button functions for Movies and TV Shows
Enable this and the Extras menu will show ratings from more services than just TMDb. Must have a valid OMDb API assigned in the Accounts settings for this to show
Choose the ratings displayed from OMDb
Enable Poster Ratings (RPDb) for Extras Lists
Choose this and the widgets displayed in the Extras window will show RPDb ratings posters
Choose this and the individual lists within the Extras window will each utilize a scrollbar when needed
Choose the Open Action when a Movie is selected in Red Light. Choices Are None, Open Extras, Open Movie Set, Both. "Both" will open the Movie Set if available or open Extras
Choose the Open Action when a TV Show is selected in Red Light. Choices Are None or Open Extras
[I]Enter an RPDb API Key in Meta Accounts[/I]
Navigate within these settings to Meta Accounts->RPDb (Ratings Posters) and enter an API key to access this
Choose which media types utilize that Rated Posters provided by RPDb. Choices are None, Movies, TV Shows or Both
Choose the format used for RPDb Posters. Choices are Default, Blocks or Rounded
[I]Enter API Keys for Google/Groq for access[/I]
Navigate to Meta Accounts to enter your Google AI and/or Groq AI API Keys for access to this feature

Choose the order AI Models will be used for Similar Titles. Top will be used first, bottom used last. You will normally want to keep this order as default. If you don't have an API Key for the model listed, it will be skipped
Choose the maximum number of AI Similar Results returned by Red Light
Enable this and Red Light will attempt to paginate lists when possible. This is mainly applicable to personal lists
Choose the limit of pagination when within Red Light
Choose the limit of pagination for Red Light widgets
Enable this and Red Light include a "Jump To..." item at the end of each page of paginated personal lists. You can use this to jump to a specific page in the list
Enable this and Red Light will ignore leading words like "The", "A" when sorting lists
Service used for Because You Watched
Choose which service provides recommendations for the Because You Watched menus. Options are Recommended (TMDb), More Like This (IMDb) or AI Similar (AI)
Seeds used for Because You Watched Random
Choose how much previously watched media is used for recommendations for the Because You Watched Random menu.
Choose the region from which the MPAA advisories will be displayed
Choose the amount of time generic lists like Trending or Popular are cached
Choose where fully watched, but still airing TV Shows are shown. Options are Watched, In Progress or Both
Enable this and Red Light will show TV Show Special Episodes
Enable this and Red Light will use the name of a season when availbale.
Choose the times when Red Light will load all episodes of a TV Show when browsed into
Enable this and Red Light will hide episode thumb and episode synopsis within Red Light
Include Anime in TV Show Watched Progress
Enable this and Red Light will include Anime content in your Watched, Progress, Progress Episodes and Next Episodes menus in the main TV Show menu
Include Unaired Media in Trakt Watchlists
Enable this and Red Light will display any unaired media in your Trakt Movie/TV Show Watchlists. Disable this and any unaired Media will be hidden
Include Adult Results from Media Searches

Enable this and Red Light will attempt to filter out adult content from Movie, TV Show and People searches. Success is based purely on the accuracy of TMDbs database. Expect that some adult results may come through and plan accordingly

Enable this and Red Light will control view types used within the addon. This can be disabled if the skin you are using has a helper that provides a "lock view" type function. If enabled, views can be set in Tools->Set Views within the addon
Enable this to manually enter values for the viewtypes used. Only do this if you know the viewtype values for the skin you are using. Otherwise, use the "Set Views" menu in Tools
Enter the Viewtype ID for Menu Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for Movies Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for TV Shows Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for Seasons Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for Episodes Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for Episode Lists Content
Enter the Viewtype ID for Premium Files Content
Contents Sort Order For Watched Progress
Choose the sort order for your In Progress media
Choose the sort order for your Watched media
Contents Sort Order For Trakt Lists
Choose the sort order for your Trakt Collection media
Choose the sort order for your Trakt Watchlist media
Contents Sort Order For TMDb Lists
Choose the sort order for your TMDb Watchlist media
Choose the sort order for your TMDb Favorites media
Choose the order in which your TMDb lists are presented. This is not for the contents of the lists, but rather the lists themselves
Choose the order in which your TMDb lists are presented. This is not for the contents of the lists, but rather the lists themselves
Enable this and Red Light will display the Author of the Personal List
Enable this and Red Light will sort all unseen Personal Lists to the top
Enable this and Red Light will highlight Unseen Personal Lists using the chosen highlight
Choose Unseen Personal Lists Highlight Color
Choose whether Red Light refreshes widgets based on a set timer. You should set a value here if you are using random widgets from Red Light, and wish for their contents to update between Kodi starts
Enable this and Red Light will show a notification whenever widgets are refreshed by the timer
Enable this and watched items will be hidden in Red Light widgets
Hide Next Page Item in Widgets
Enable this and the "Next Page" item will be hidden in Red Light widgets
[I]Enter an RPDb API Key in Meta Accounts[/I]
Navigate within these settings to Meta Accounts->RPDb (Ratings Posters) and enter an API key to access this
Choose which media types utilize that Rated Posters provided by RPDb. Choices are None, Movies, TV Shows or Both
Choose the format used for RPDb Posters. Choices are Default, Blocks or Rounded
Choose Content for the Context Menu
Choose what content is included within the Context Menu
Choose Order for the Context Menu
Choose how the content within the Context Menu is ordered
Single Episode Display Format - Within Addon
Choose the display format Red Light uses when naming single episodes within the addon. Applicable for lists like Next Episodes, In Progress Episodes etc
Choose the display format Red Light uses when naming single episodes for widgets. Applicable for lists like Next Episodes, In Progress Episodes etc
Enable this to share with Kodi the number of unwatched episodes for the TV Show of each episode in single episode lists
Choose the method used to determine the content of your Next Episodes list. Last Aired or Last Watched
Choose the sort type of Next Episodes list. Recently Watched, Airdate or Title
Choose the sort direction of Next Episodes list. Ascending or Descending
Enable this to limit the amount of watched history used to determine Next Episodes

A setting of "20" will limit the history used based on the previous 20 watched episodes. This means a 20 limit will not usually result in 20 items in your Next Episodes list, rather the previous 20 watched episodes will be used as history
Choose which lists are included within Red Lights Next Episodes. You can include your Trakt Watchlist, your Red Light Favorites, or both. They will show with a DARKGOLDENROD highlight
Enable this to include the airdate at the beginning of the list item label
Enable this to sort episodes airing today to the top of the Next Episodes list
Include Unaired Episodes When Within 7 Days
Enable this to include unaired episodes within Next Episodes. They will show with a RED highlight and become visible within 7 days of their airdate
Flatten TV Shows Airing on Same Day
Enable this to limit to the first instance of a TV Show episode when that TV Show has multiple episodes airing on the same day
Choose the sort direction of the Trakt Calendar list. Ascending or Descending
Choose how many previous days will be shown within the Trakt Calendar
Choose how many future days will be shown within the Trakt Calendar
Authorize your Trakt account and use it within Red Light
Revoke authorization of your Trakt account
Enter here your Trakt Client ID key
Restore default Trakt Client ID Key
Enter here your Trakt Client Secret key
Restore default Trakt Client Secret Key
Enter here your TMDb API key
Test if your TMDb API is activated and working

Enter your TMDb v4 read access token (JWT). Used for TMDb Lists account authorization and API calls before you sign in with Authorize Account. Create one at themoviedb.org/settings/api with API Read Access permission.
- Restore Default Lists Read Access Token
Restore default TMDb Lists read access token
Test if your TMDb Lists read access token is valid
Authorize your TMDb account and use it within Red Light for Lists
Revoke authorization of your TMDb account
Enter here your OMDb API key. This will allow you to utilize extra ratings information in Red Lights Extras window
Enter here your RPDb API key. This will allow you to utilize posters that include built in ratings information when available
Enter here your Gemini/Gemma AI API key. This will allow you to utilize the features within Red Light that use AI, such as Browse AI Similar
Enter here your Groq AI API key. This will allow you to utilize the features within Red Light that use AI, such as Browse AI Similar
Enable this to use external scrapers. No support is provided when you choose to use external scrapers within Red Light
Choose the external scraper module to use within Red Light. No support is provided when you choose to use external scrapers within Red Light
Open the External Scraper settings. The settings of the module chosen above will be opened
Authorize your All Debrid account and use it within Red Light
Enable the use of your All Debrid account
Revoke authorization of your All Debrid account
When enabled, Red Light checks whether external torrent results are cached on your All Debrid account before listing them. You may see fewer links, but those shown should be instant.
Add Resolved Debrid Cache to Cloud
Choose which resolved media is automatically added to your All Debrid Cloud. All media or only show packs
Enable this and Red Light will search your All Debrid Cloud for media
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching functions on your All Debrid Cloud results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable this and Red Light will search for results in your All Debrid Cloud before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from the All Debrid Cloud
Enable this and Red Light will sort the results from your All Debrid Cloud to the top of all results
Choose the order in which All Debrid results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Enable the use of your EasyNews account
Enter the Login of your EasyNews account
Enter the Password of your EasyNews account

After EasyNews returns results, match filenames against the movie or episode title (and aliases). EasyNews search is already narrow (e.g. Movie Year or Show S01E05), so this often removes few extra results but helps when unrelated files slip through. Disable if valid links go missing. Search Width, Filter Languages, and Fallback Search Queries usually matter more for EasyNews
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra language filtering on your EasyNews results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable to request Safe Search filtering from EasyNews and exclude adult results from EasyNews queries.
Choose which languages you wish to include in EasyNews results
When language filtering is enabled, keep files EasyNews does not report a language for. Disable to exclude them (strict filtering)

Reload your EasyNews username and password from Red Light settings before every Solr search and before resolving or downloading a file. Kodi normally keeps the login in memory for the session; enable this if you change your EasyNews password on the website, edit credentials in settings, or share a profile and want the next search to use the updated login without restarting Kodi. Expect a small extra delay per request when enabled

If the main Search Width queries return no files, run extra EasyNews searches using TMDB alternate titles (aliases). For TV: alias plus episode (e.g. Show S01E05) and alias plus season (Show S01). For movies: alias plus release year. Only queries not already tried are used. Helpful when EasyNews filenames use a different show name than TMDB. Usually unnecessary if Search Width is Broad, because Broad already searches aliases. Default is on

How many EasyNews Solr searches to run per movie or episode. Results are merged and duplicates removed. Focused (fastest): one query only — Movie: Title Year (e.g. Dune 2021); TV: Title S01E05 (e.g. Breaking Bad S01E05). Balanced: Focused plus a wider query — Movie: title without year; TV: Title S01 (all episodes in that season on EasyNews). Broad (slowest, best recall): Balanced plus show or movie title only, then the same patterns for each TMDB alias. Broad pulls many unrelated files for short titles; keep Filter Results by Name enabled. More width means more API calls and a longer source search

Query EasyNews early in the source search, before external torrent scrapers and the rest of the internal scrape list. The progress window can show EasyNews quality counts (4K, 1080p, etc.) while torrent scrapers are still running. If EasyNews returns results during this prescrape step, you may get playable links sooner instead of waiting for the full scrape to finish. Does not replace external scrapers — it runs EasyNews first, then the normal search continues. Often used in conjunction with Autoplay Result, below (when Check Before Full Search is enabled).
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from EasyNews
Choose the order in which EasyNews results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Authorize your Offcloud account with a QR code and use it within Red Light
Enable the use of your Offcloud account
Revoke authorization of your Offcloud account
When enabled, Red Light checks whether external torrent results are cached on your Offcloud account before listing them. You may see fewer links, but those shown should be instant.
When enabled, torrents not in Offcloud instant cache also appear in the main results list (subject to minimum seeders). Selecting an uncached row adds it to your Offcloud cloud.
Add Resolved Debrid Cache to Cloud
Choose which resolved media is automatically added to your Offcloud cloud. All media or only show packs
Notify when added torrents are ready in Cloud
Notify when a magnet you added to Offcloud (uncached Add to Cloud, context menu, or kept via Store Resolved to Cloud) has finished and is available in Cloud Storage / History
Enable this and Red Light will search your Offcloud cloud for media
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching on your Offcloud cloud results
Enable this and Red Light will search your Offcloud cloud before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will sort Offcloud cloud results to the top of all results
Choose the order in which Offcloud results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Authorize your Premiumize account and use it within Red Light
Enable the use of your Premiumize account
Revoke authorization of your Premiumize account
When enabled, Red Light checks whether external torrent results are cached on your Premiumize account before listing them. You may see fewer links, but those shown should be instant.
When enabled, torrents not in Premiumize instant cache also appear in the main results list (subject to minimum seeders). Selecting an uncached row adds it to your Premiumize cloud.
Add Resolved Debrid Cache to Cloud
Choose which resolved media is automatically added to your Premiumize Cloud. All media or only show packs
Enable this and Red Light will search your Premiumize Cloud for media
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching functions on your Premiumize Cloud results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable this and Red Light will search for results in your Premiumize Cloud before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from the Premiumize Cloud
Enable this and Red Light will sort the results from your Premiumize Cloud to the top of all results
Choose the order in which Premiumize results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Authorize your Real Debrid account and use it within Red Light
Enable the use of your Real Debrid account
Revoke authorization of your Real Debrid account
When enabled, Red Light checks whether external torrent results are cached on your Real Debrid account before listing them. You may see fewer links, but those shown should be instant.
Add Resolved Debrid Cache to Cloud
Choose which resolved media is automatically added to your Real Debrid Cloud. All media or only show packs
Enable this and Red Light will search your Real Debrid Cloud for media
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching functions on your Real Debrid Cloud results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable this and Red Light will search for results in your Real Debrid Cloud before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from the Real Debrid Cloud
Enable this and Red Light will sort the results from your Real Debrid Cloud to the top of all results
Choose the order in which Real Debrid results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Enable this and Red Light will use an alternate base url for RD. This may circumvent any blocks being performed

When enabled, Red Light removes one active Real Debrid torrent before adding a new one if you already have 5 active downloads (RD's usual limit). This can prevent failed resolves when slots are full, but may delete a transfer you started elsewhere — including in the RD website or another app. Leave disabled unless you often hit resolve failures due to too many active downloads.
Authorize your TorBox account with a QR code and use it within Red Light
Enable the use of your TorBox account
Revoke authorization of your TorBox account
When enabled, Red Light checks whether external torrent results are cached on your TorBox account before listing them. You may see fewer links, but those shown should be instant.
When enabled, torrents not in TorBox shared cache also appear in the main results list (subject to minimum seeders). Selecting an uncached row adds it to your TorBox cloud.
Add Resolved Debrid Cache to Cloud
Choose which resolved media is automatically added to your TorBox Cloud. All media or only show packs
Notify when added torrents are ready in Cloud
Notify when a torrent you added to TorBox (uncached Add to Cloud, or kept via Store Resolved to Cloud) has finished and is available in Cloud Storage / History
Enable this and Red Light will search your TorBox Cloud for media before external scrapers run.
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching functions on your TorBox Cloud results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable this and Red Light will search for results in your TorBox Cloud before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from the TorBox Cloud
Enable this and Red Light will sort the results from your TorBox Cloud to the top of all results
Choose the order in which TorBox results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Enable AIOStreams as an optional source provider alongside Debrid, EasyNews, and external scrapers. Requires your AIOStreams instance username and password (HTTP Basic Auth).

Each preset shows its base URL in the picker and in this row. Kuu = aiostreams.stremio.ru | ElfHosted = aiostreams.elfhosted.com | Yeb = aiostreams.fortheweak.cloud | Midnight = aiostreamsfortheweebsstable.midnightignite.me | Custom = your own host (set URL below).
The base URL Red Light uses for /api/v1/search on this scrape. Updates when you change Instance or Custom URL. API calls go to this host plus /api/v1/search.
Base URL for your custom AIOStreams instance (no trailing slash), e.g. https://aiostreams.example.com
HTTP Basic Auth username for your AIOStreams instance.
HTTP Basic Auth password for your AIOStreams instance.
After AIOStreams returns results, match filenames against the movie or episode title (and aliases). Disable if valid links go missing.
Query AIOStreams early in the source search, before external torrent scrapers finish. Does not replace Debrid, EasyNews, or external scrapers. Often used with Autoplay Result below.
When Check Before Full Search is enabled, autoplay the top AIOStreams result if one is found during prescrape.
Choose the order in which AIOStreams results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order.
Enable the use of any local media folder locations i.e. NAS drives, HDDs etc
Choose this to activate a settings window allowing you to set the location of up to 5 devices containing local media
Enable this and Red Light will perform extra name matching functions on your local media folders results. Will help to limit incorrect results, but may also filter some relevant results
Enable this and Red Light will search for results from your local media folders before checking other enabled scrapers
Enable this and Red Light will Autoplay the top result found from Local Folders
Enable this and Red Light will sort the results from your local media folders to the top of all results
Enable this and Red Light will ignore any Quality or Size filters when processing local media folders results
Choose the order in which Media Folders results appear in relation to any other active accounts. The lower the number, the higher the sort order
Choose the amount of time Red Light will allow all scrapers to search for results
Choose the format in which Red Light will display the results of a source search. Lists, Rows and WideList are provided

Configure automatic retries when a source search returns no playable results. Each action can be Off, Auto, or Prompt, with order from Highest to Lowest. Rescrape With No Cache Check disables per-provider external cache checks (Real Debrid, All Debrid, TorBox, Premiumize, Offcloud). Rescrape With Disabled External Providers includes disabled external torrent providers (not internal scrapers such as EasyNews or debrid cloud). Offer Full Search After Early Results applies to the empty-search retry path only; Check Before Full Search and Run External Scraper Search during prescrape are separate.
Choose the priority ordering performed by Red Light before displaying source results
Choose the method used to filter results by their size. Either by internet speed, or actual size of the result

Enter here your internet line speed. It is a good idea to choose a value slightly below your true internet speed, although Red Light will still reduce this value by 10% before filtering results by size
Enter here the Maximum size allowed for Movies playback
Enter here the Maximum size allowed for Episodes playback
Enter here the Minimum size allowed for Movies
Enter here the Minimum size allowed for Episodes
Enable this and Red Light will include results where the size of the file is unknown
Use Weighted Value for Size Sorting
Enable this and Red Light will weight HEVC file sizes as being equal to 2x a non-HEVC file size when sorting results
Choose the sort direction with regards to results size
Enter here the Minimum Seeders a result must have to be displayed by Red Light

Choose whether Red Light limits the number of results shown. If a number is selected, each Quality in the results will be limited to that number of results. For example, if you choose 20 Per Quality, then Red Light will display 20x4K results, 20x1080p results etc
Choose whether Red Light limits the number of results shown
Choose how to handle Pre-Release source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle 3D source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle HDR source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle DOLBY VISION source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle AV1 source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle AI Enhanced/Upscaled source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose how to handle HEVC source results. They can be INCLUDED or EXCLUDED
Choose the maximum quality allowed for HEVC files when allowing source select
Choose the maximum quality allowed for HEVC files being autoplayed
Choose when to sort certain results to the top. The options are NONE, SOURCE SELECT, AUTOPLAY or BOTH
- Set Filters for Sort to Top

Choose video and audio properties that will be sorted to the top of the sources list. Choose up to 5 different parameters to be used, in order of importance. "Sort to Top" settings involving Debrid Cache and Folder Scraper results will take priority over these 5 params
Choose audio properties that will be filtered out of source results
Choose the method used to provide highlight colors to source results. The options are PROVIDER, QUALITY, or the use of a SINGLE COLOR
Choose Single color for all results
Enable this for Movies to play automatically when selected
Choose the Quality properties allowed for source select results
Choose the Quality properties allowed for autoplay results
Choose when Red Light will automatically resume playback of an in-progress Movie
Enable this and Red Light will show an alert towards the end of a movie if the movie contains during or after credit stingers
Choose when Red Light will show the Credit Stingers Alert
- Use Chapter Info For Alert When Available

Enable this and Red Light will attempt to use video file Chapter information to ascertain when to show the Credit Stingers Alert. If unsuccessful, then the percentage of playback value will be used instead
Enable this for Episodes to play automatically when selected
Choose the Quality properties allowed for source select results
Choose the Quality properties allowed for autoplay results
Enable this and Red Light will automatically play the next episode of a TV Show at the end of the episode currently being watched
Choose the alert method when Next Episode is ready to be played. Choose Window or Notification
Choose the action performed when you fail to interact with the Next Episode alert
Choose when Red Light will show the Next Episode Alert
- Use Chapter Info For Alert When Available

Enable this and Red Light will attempt to use video file Chapter information to ascertain when to show the Next Episode alert. If unsuccessful, then the percentage of playback value will be used instead
Choose after how many auto played episodes Red Light will ask if you are still watching. Set to 0 to disable
Enable this and Red Light will automatically search for sources for the next episode of a TV Show towards the end of the episode currently being watched
Choose when Red Light will show the Next Episode Alert
- Use Chapter Info For Alert When Available

Enable this and Red Light will attempt to use video file Chapter information to ascertain when to show the Next Episode alert. If unsuccessful, then the percentage of playback value will be used instead
Enable this and Red Light will confirm you wish to autoscrape the next episode towards the end of the current eisodes runtime
Choose when Red Light will automatically resume playback of an in-progress Episode
Limit Resolve Attempts to Selected Result

Enable this and Red Light will limit the playback attempts to only the source result chosen. If disabled, Red Light will potentially try 15 results of similar properties to your chosen source result until it is able to successfully resolve and playback

Choose the method for Red Light to use to attempt optimal EasyNews playback. RETRY will retry once the link if it fails to resolve (slow). NO SEEK will tell Kodi the stream is not seekable. This results in quick and consistent resolving of the stream, but you will be unable to seek the stream outside of the cached data. Both is to use both methods. None is to use none of the methods.
Choose the maximum retries allowed for EasyNews playback attempts
- Exclude NO SEEK Playback for My Services
Enable this and Red Light will not use the NO SEEK EasyNews Playback Method when resolving regular files from the My Services->EasyNews location
Check Volume Before Start of Playback
Enable this and Red Light will attempt to check the current level of volume within Kodi, and adjust it downwards if it is exceeding a set level
Choose the maximum allowed volume level
Enable subtitle automation in playback. When on, choose Local Subtitles or SubMaker (https://submaker.elfhosted.com)
Choose Local Subtitles or SubMaker. Local only enables subtitles already in the stream. SubMaker (https://submaker.elfhosted.com) fetches subtitles using your manifest URL below
Choose the preferred subtitle language for SubMaker. Default is English
When using SubMaker, check for local subtitles first. If local subtitles are active, Red Light will keep them and skip SubMaker download
Enter your full SubMaker manifest URL from https://submaker.elfhosted.com (must include manifest.json). Example: https://.../manifest.json
